label: Wireless Security Specialist
description: Specializes in wireless network security assessment — WPA2/WPA3 attacks, enterprise wireless, rogue access points, Bluetooth attacks, and wireless intrusion detection.

paths.name: Wireless Security Track
paths.rationale: OSWP, GAWN, and CWSP cover wireless security from practical exploitation through advanced attacks to enterprise wireless architecture.
pros: OSWP proves hands-on wireless attack capability
GAWN covers advanced attacks beyond OSWP scope
CWSP adds enterprise wireless architecture knowledge
cons: Thin employer demand specifically for wireless certs vs broader security roles
OSWP curriculum criticized as dated regarding WPA3 and enterprise wireless

prerequisites.experience: Requires dedicated wireless testing hardware — a compatible USB wireless adapter with monitor mode and injection support.

cert_sequence.notes: OffSec wireless pentesting cert. WEP/WPA/WPA2 attacks, fake access points, client attacks. 3-hour practical exam. Complete PEN-210 course. Prepare a hardware lab with two compatible wireless adapters.
GIAC wireless security cert — advanced attacks beyond OSWP including enterprise wireless (WPA2-Enterprise/802.1X) and Bluetooth attacks. Open-book. Associated with SANS SEC617. DoD 8140 approved.

CWNP enterprise wireless security cert covering WLAN security policies and wireless intrusion prevention. More defensive and architecture-focused — adds enterprise design perspective for network security engineer roles with wireless scope.
